problem: Given $\begin{vmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{vmatrix} = 5,$ find $\begin{vmatrix} 2a & 2b \\ 2c & 2d \end{vmatrix}.$

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of a determinant?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain how the determinant of a 2x2 matrix is calculated?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How is the determinant affected when all the elements in a row or a column of the matrix are multiplied by the same constant?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: If the determinant of a matrix is zero, what does that tell us about the matrix?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Given the determinant of a 2x2 matrix, can you find the values of its elements?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the determinant change when the rows or columns of a matrix are interchanged?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Using the properties of determinants, can you explain how to find the determinant of the matrix $\begin{vmatrix} 2a & 2b \\ 2c & 2d \end{vmatrix}$ in terms of the determinant of the matrix $\begin{vmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{vmatrix}$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you use the property mentioned in the previous question to find the determinant of the matrix $\begin{vmatrix} 3 & 4 \\ 6 & 8 \end{vmatrix}$ using the determinant of the matrix $\begin{vmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{vmatrix}$?
classified_questions.label: b
